claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm inquiring about. His name is Alex, and he's 21 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 21 years old, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training, but it typically stops once they turn 20.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school a while back and isn't studying anymore.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I now have enough details to assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 21 years old
- He is not in approved education or training

According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. The benefit can continue if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old and not in approved education or training, he is beyond the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.

Let me record this outcome for you:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex. Since he is 21 years old, he is over the age limit for Child Benefit. The benefit typically stops when a child turns 16, unless they continue in approved education or training - in which case it can continue until they turn 20. As Alex is now 21, he is beyond the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility.

Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for children under 16. For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit can continue if they stay in approved education or training, but this only extends until they turn 20. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of his education or training status.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Alex is above the maximum age threshold of 20 for Child Benefit, making the claimant ineligible for Child Benefit for this child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for children under 16. For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit can continue if they stay in approved education or training, but this only extends until they turn 20. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of his education or training status.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Alex is above the maximum age threshold of 20 for Child Benefit, making the claimant ineligible for Child Benefit for this child.
